title: Kernel orthestrationについて、codexだけでなくglmやclaudeとの協奏として設定されているか確認せよ
summary_text: - `Critical`: enforcement is not universal. Multi-model guard/bootstrapping is only applied for `kernel|vote|v`; other prompts fall through to direct `codex` execution. This blocks any blanket “all non-trivial work” claim. [codex-prompt-launch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L225) [codex-prompt-launch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L286) - `Critical`: fail-closed can be disabled by env toggles (`CODEX_KERNEL_REQUIRE_GUARD`, `CODEX_KERNEL_USE_GUARD_LAUNCH`, `CODEX_KERNEL_PROVIDER_CHECK`). If these are flipped, single-lane fallback is possible and becomes a workflow violation. [codex-prompt-launch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L81) [codex-prompt-launch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L86) [codex-prompt-launch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L91) - `High`: provider gate proof is not run-scoped. It uses global `active-model-lanes.jsonl` over a 60-minute window and only checks provider presence (`claude`,`glm`), so stale/other-session records can satisfy the gate. [task_backup.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/task_backup.py#L22) [task_backup.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/task_backup.py#L490) [task_backup.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/task_backup.py#L531) [cli.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/cli.py#L458) - `High`: lane policy enforces multi-agent counts, not strict multi-model execution. `lane_family` comes from spawn arguments (`lane_family`/`provider`/`agent_type`) and threshold checks are family/count-based. [session_watch.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/session_watch.py#L124) [session_watch.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/session_watch.py#L135) [session_watch.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/session_watch.py#L267) - `Medium`: local orchestration still has policy-off switches (`policy-disabled` paths), so baseline trio enforcement is not immutable. [run-local-orchestration.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/local/run-local-orchestration.sh#L629) [run-local-orchestration.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/local/run-local-orchestration.sh#L961) [run-local-orchestration.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/local/run-local-orchestration.sh#L952) - `Medium`: GLM readiness check is still static (`script exists + key`) in bridge status; no live GLM probe in `--check`, so orchestration may start with GLM effectively down. [kernel-lane-bridge.mjs](/Users/masayuki/Dev/kernel-orchestration-tools/kernel-lane-bridge.mjs#L756) [kernel-lane-bridge.mjs](/Users/masayuki/Dev/kernel-orchestration-tools/kernel-lane-bridge.mjs#L918) Net: after latest changes, you can claim “guarded Kernel path can enforce multi-lane under default settings,” but not “all non-trivial work is automatically multi-model.”
